overview: This small wild cat is a margay (Leopardus wiedii), often mistaken for an ocelot. Margays are highly arboreal, uniquely adapted to live in trees with hind ankles that can rotate 180 degrees to climb down headfirst. They are native to the tropical and subtropical forests of Central and South America. The species is currently listed as Near Threatened on the IUCN Red List due to habitat loss. Margays are highly arboreal, uniquely adapted to live in trees with hind ankles that can rotate 180 degrees to climb down headfirst.
They are native to the tropical and subtropical forests of Central and South America.
The species is currently listed as Near Threatened on the IUCN Red List due to habitat loss.
